hook.text: Search 'laptop' on Amazon. Filter by brand, price, screen size, and rating. That's faceted search.

buildup.text: Facets let users narrow results by multiple attributes simultaneously instead of browsing one category at a time.

discovery.text: Faceted search was pioneered at Endeca in the early 2000s. It transformed e-commerce by making large catalogs browsable.

twist.text: Too many facets overwhelm. Showing 20 filter options is worse than showing 5. Prioritize the most-used facets first.

climax.text: The best implementations show facets with result counts. 'Brand: Apple (347)' tells users the filter will actually work.

punchline.text: Let users filter by what matters. Show result counts beside each option.

quiz.question: Why should faceted search show result counts next to each filter option?
options: Users can see how many results match, avoiding dead-end filters
Result counts make the page load faster
Numbers are more attractive than text labels
